what_it_does: This file is the single-source-of-truth model capability catalog, keyed by model ID (HF/cloud string). Each entry records what a model can do — tools, JSON-schema constrained decode, thinking/reasoning, context ceiling, concurrency knee, speed profile — with every field tagged by provenance (declared, probed, measured, served) and a set of capability tags in `provides`.
observations: Every capability field is wrapped as `{value, source, note?}` with provenance from declared/probed/measured/served.
`provides` is a tag set grounded in hard signals: `--tool-call-parser` → tools, `chat_template_nothink` → no-think, `--runner pooling` → embed, chat model → chat/json.
The catalog distinguishes three populations: currently served vLLM models, on-disk-not-yet-served models, and cloud-only models.
VRAM and GPU utilization are explicitly excluded; the file stores only intrinsic capabilities and joins service-layer data elsewhere.
Unknowns are recorded honestly with `value: null` and `source: declared` rather than fabricated.
The `kimi-k2.7-code:cloud` entry is probed (not merely declared) for tools, thinking, and context_ceiling=262144, and its note ties it to a runtime context-pick rule.
Non-chat models are included: embedding models, rerankers, TTS engines, STT engines, a vision layout detector, and an audio codec with an empty `provides`.
Some entries have multiple quant/variant siblings (e.g., Orpheus AWQ/full-precision/w8a8) for inventory completeness.
standouts: Provenance-attested schema: every capability value carries a source, making verification status explicit.
Served/on-disk/cloud taxonomy with honest nulls for unverified capabilities.
`kimi-k2.7-code:cloud` is a live-probed cloud reasoner explicitly marked as the context-pick boundary (262144) with a fallback to `deepseek-v4-flash:cloud`.
An audio codec (`hubertsiuzdak/snac_24khz`) is cataloged with an empty `provides` because it is not role-bindable.
conventions: Each model entry begins with a `_note` summarizing its status and grounding.
Each capability is an object with `value`, `source`, and often `note`.
On-disk-not-yet-served models are flagged as such in `_note` and use `source: declared`.
Cloud models consistently note the absence of a local VRAM slot.
Capability tags in `provides` are derived from concrete service flags or model-card facts, not inferred from model name.
concerns: Many `json_schema` values are `null` even for model families where support is likely, because they are unverified on that exact ID.
Most `concurrency_knee` and `speed_profile` values are `null` or copied from launch config rather than measured.
The `kimi-k2.7-code:cloud` note references external runtime functions (`runtime/cc_clone.pick_ollama_model_for_context`, `fabric.config.OLLAMA_DIRECT`) that are not resolvable from this file alone.
Large cloud context ceilings are declared from `ollama show` without evidence of effective usable context.
notes: A provenance-heavy capability registry that tells the swarm which model-ID can satisfy which tags, with explicit honesty about unknowns and a special probed entry for kimi-k2.7-code:cloud used as a context-pick boundary.
questions: What is the exact `CAPABILITY_TAGS` vocabulary referenced by `provides`?
How does `cli/capabilities.py` fail loud when a model is missing or the file is malformed?
What is the precise JOIN mechanism with `services.json` and `gpu.py`?
What do 'TIM-RULE' and the runtime function `pick_ollama_model_for_context` actually implement?
How is a `probed` source validated and refreshed over time?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — a single-source, provenance-attested capability catalog for model selection — matches what the file does.
apparent_themes: Epistemic humility via provenance and explicit nulls
Capability tags over raw hardware specs
Separation of intrinsic capabilities from service-layer resource data
intention_signals: The file is trying to make model capability data auditable, source-attested, and honest about verification status so agents do not assume capabilities that have not been grounded.
novelty: The provenance-laden schema with explicit nulls and per-field notes is unusual, as is the inclusion of non-bindable inventory (audio codec) and on-disk-not-yet-served models alongside live-served and cloud entries.
contribution: It contributes a structured, source-attested capability vocabulary that a fused system can use to route roles to models without guessing, while clearly marking what is verified, declared, or unknown.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON catalog keyed by model ID that records declared, probed, measured, or served capabilities — tools, JSON schema, thinking, context ceiling, concurrency, speed, and provides tags — for local, on-disk, and cloud models used by the company ops runtime.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single source of truth for what each model can do, with provenance and explicit unknowns, so agent role binding and model selection can be grounded rather than assumed.
